summary: Fixed 12 of 15 findings: 5 silent catches, 4 inconsistent error types, 3 inconsistent logging. Deferred Q-06 (stream cancel — acceptable as-is), Q-12 (batch endpoint TODO — tracked in backlog), Q-13/Q-14 (as-any in external API adapters — acceptable).

fixes.description: Added log.warn with error details before setting empty fallback in TTS voices/models catch blocks. Uses existing createLogger('voice') instance.
Added console.warn with '[about]' prefix in all three .catch() blocks for storage info, task count, and plugin count loading.
Added console.warn with '[filter-view]' prefix before setting savedFilter to null on error.
Added console.warn with '[ai-settings]' prefix in model fetch failure catch block.
Replaced empty comment with console.warn('[microphone]') for microphone permission query failures.

fixes.reason: Stream cancel errors are expected and benign. Report marked as acceptable as-is.

fixes.description: Changed 'throw new Error(...)' to 'throw new ValidationError(...)' for cycle detection. ValidationError already imported in this file.

Added import for NotFoundError and ValidationError from core/errors.ts. Changed 3 throws: 'Plugin not found' → NotFoundError, 'no default export' → ValidationError, 'cannot remove built-in' → ValidationError.
Added createLogger('ai-sessions') import and replaced both console.warn calls with log.warn using structured { error } data object.
Added createLogger('filters') and replaced 2 console.error calls with log.error using structured { error } data object.
Added createLogger('settings') and replaced console.warn + console.error with log.warn/log.error using structured data objects.

fixes.reason: Batch endpoint TODO (PERF-301) is a performance optimization tracked in backlog, not a quality fix.
as-any casts in piper-local-tts.ts interact with external WASM libraries lacking TypeScript types. Acceptable.
as-any casts for vendor-prefixed Web Speech API. Standard browser pattern, acceptable.
Broad console.* vs createLogger migration is too large for one round. Three key modules (Q-09, Q-10, Q-11) were migrated as representative examples.
